extraction_method: Visual reading of scanned PDF pages 29-41 via claude-doc-tools image export
note: The AiG GenCon preview does NOT contain a dedicated Equipment chapter (it was planned but never published). Starting equipment is derived from: 1) Combat style weapon lists per culture, 2) Cultural narrative descriptions of technology level. Starting money formulas are in the Characters chapter p.24-25. Social class determines equipment quality per Mythras Core p.21-24 and p.33-34. Combat style weapon lists are linked by CSE IDs because Combat Styles Encyclopedia supersedes AiG for combat styles only.
equipment_chapter_status: NOT PRESENT in GenCon 2015 preview. Referenced as 'Economics and Equipment' but was among chapters noted as incomplete.

starting_money.note: 1 Lunar has substantially more buying power than the default Silver Piece of RuneQuest

currency_system.buying_power_note: AiG states the Lunar has substantially more buying power than the RuneQuest Silver Piece.
currency_system.attestation_note: Wheel, Clack, Bolg, and cow-value conversion ratios are not visible on verified AiG pages 24-25 and are not promoted here.

Balazaring.technology_note: Hunters who use bow and spear. Most weapons and tools crafted from wood and stone. Occupying Lunars have made bronze more available. Richest Balazarings revel in owning metal weapons.

Esrolian.technology_note: Urbanised Orlanthi with mature bronze-working. Well-equipped military with formation-trained troops.

God Forgot.technology_note: Western-style culture using Brithini caste system. Holars (warriors) and Horali (mercenaries) use conventional bronze weapons. Zzistori (sorcerers) use improvised weapons.

Lunar Heartland.technology_note: Highly advanced Dara Happan civilisation with bronze and some iron. Professional military with heavy infantry, cavalry, and specialist units.

Lunar Provincial.technology_note: Provincial Lunars far from heartland. Mix of Dara Happan military equipment and local adaptations.

Praxian.technology_note: Primitive level of technology. Metal goods highly prized and must be traded, stolen, or won in battle. Weapons mostly leather, bone, and wood. Riding beast tribes use Combat Style traits like Skirmisher when mounted.

Sartarite (Heortling).technology_note: Sophisticated crafters and metalworkers. Learned art of metalworking from the dwarves (god Gustbran). Bronze weapons standard, iron rare and prized.

Telmori Hsunchen.technology_note: Neolithic hunter-gatherers with little or no technology. Simple weapons of wood, stone, and bone. In wolf form, use teeth and claws.

social_status_by_culture.note: Social class determines starting equipment quality. Use the table from Mythras Core (Barbarian table p.31 for Sartarite, appropriate culture type table for others).

starting_equipment_derivation.method: AiG does not provide explicit starting equipment lists. Starting gear is derived from:
steps: 1. Roll starting money per culture formula
2. Determine social class (Mythras Core p.21-24, culture-appropriate table)
3. Character receives weapons from their chosen combat style
4. Armour and other equipment purchased from starting money using Mythras Core equipment prices (p.69-72)
5. Cultural technology level constrains available materials (e.g., Balazaring: stone/wood; Sartarite: bronze; Lunar: bronze/some iron)
starting_equipment_derivation.attestation_note: This derivation method is attested by AiG p.24-25 ('Determine Starting Money and Equipment') which gives only the money formula and directs players to purchase equipment. The combat style weapons are the character's starting weapons per standard Mythras character creation (Core p.33: 'Characters begin with weapons and a shield as noted in their Combat Style').

validation_method: Pre-Magic PDF validation plus CSE supersession for combat styles.
